prompt: Create a hyper-realistic kawaii confectionery artwork: a chocolate-covered banana on a wooden popsicle stick, shaped like a curved banana and placed diagonally on an ornate vintage porcelain plate. The dessert is decorated entirely with thousands of tiny round sugar beads forming an anime-style girl illustration on the banana surface. The girl has caramel brown long flowing hair, large glossy brown eyes, soft blush, a small gentle smile, and a white fluffy off-shoulder dress tied with a tiny black ribbon at the chest. Make the bead mosaic look tactile and three-dimensional, with every strand of hair, face shading, eyes, dress folds, and shadows rendered from individual sugar pearls. The chocolate shell is dark glossy brown and visible around the edges and at the top and bottom, with extra decorations: pastel flower sprinkles, tiny gold stars, white sugar pearls, blue beads, pink beads, and small metallic dragees scattered like a fancy dessert topping. Count the main visible elements as one decorated chocolate banana, one wooden stick, one anime girl bead portrait, one ornate porcelain plate, two piping bags or decorating tubes, two metal tweezers, and five small open jars or dishes of sugar beads in the background containing pearl white, pink, pale blue, beige-gold, and pastel pink bead colors. Surround the plate with a warm craft-table setting: spilled pearls, tiny flower sprinkles, blurred glass jars, a soft wooden tabletop, and shallow depth of field. Use macro food photography lighting, warm natural highlights, creamy bokeh, high detail, realistic reflections on chocolate, delicate handmade patisserie mood, no text, no watermark, no extra characters.
